preamble: The phonetic transformation from a voiceless velar plosive ([k]) to a voiceless labiodental fricative ([f]) represents a significant sound change involving both a shift in the place of articulation from velar to labiodental and a change in the manner of articulation from a stop to a fricative. This type of lenition, specifically spirantization, is attested in the historical development of certain Japonic languages, particularly within the Southern Ryukyuan branch.
phoneticEffects: Lenition, Spirantization, Place Assimilation (velar to labiodental)
sources: Lau, T. (2015). Reevaluating the Diphthong Mergers in Japono-Ryukyuan. *Proceedings of the Forty-First Annual Meeting of the Berkeley Linguistics Society*, 7-8.
Bentley, J. R. (2008). *A Linguistic History of the Forgotten Islands: A Reconstruction of the Proto-Language of the Southern Ryūkyūs*. Brill.
